original_abstract:
To what extent do the principles of church revitalization align with the transformative work of the Holy Spirit? This article posits that the Holy Spirit serves as the primary catalyst for congregational renewal, suggesting that effective church leadership depends on active cooperation with the Spirit’s initiative. Within this framework, the Spirit empowers leaders to act as instruments for reviving and restoring the vitality of their faith communities. 

The discussion begins by defining spiritual empowerment through a concise survey of biblical examples, followed by an analysis of the psychological and theological principles that underpin the empowerment of church leaders. After considering practical methodologies for revitalizing declining congregations, the author concludes by identifying several distinct markers of biblical empowerment that signify a healthy, Spirit-led local church.
humanized_abstract:
Where does church revitalization play itself out in relation to the working of the Holy Spirit? Scripture teaches the Holy Spirit is the dominant Spirit in any church renewal effort and therefore leadership in any church will have to cooperate with Him in His efforts to revive and revitalize their churches. The Spirit gives these leaders His empowerment to function as His tools to accomplish His purpose of the revitalization of His churches.

This discussion starts by defining spiritual empowerment using a brief survey of biblical illustrations followed by an exploration of the psychological and theological dynamics involved in empowering church leaders. It then considers various ways in which declining churches can be revitalized before summarizing in several key indicators of biblical empowerment that provide indices of a Spirit-empowered local church.
